prompt:
Our ops boxes are zsh-only (5.9) and the crew wants the card game War replayable from fixture decks to settle a long-running argument about who'd have won lunch. Write `war.zsh` in the repo root, run as `war.zsh `. Write it zsh-NATIVE — 1-indexed arrays, no reliance on word-splitting — and pure zsh: the acceptance harness empties PATH before every invocation, so forking out to anything external fails. Deck files: one card per line (blank lines ignored), rank then suit — ranks 2-9, T, J, Q, K, A (low to high; suits C/D/H/S never break ties). Anything else -> `error: invalid card: ` on stderr, exit 2. Unreadable file -> `error: cannot read `; a deck with no cards -> `error: empty deck: `; wrong argument count -> `usage: war.zsh ` (all stderr, exit 2). Play: each round both players flip their top card. Higher rank takes every card played this round; the take goes to the BOTTOM of the winner's deck as player A's played cards in the order they hit the table, then player B's, no matter who won. Print `round N: A vs B ->

takes (A , B )` with the post-round deck counts, A always listed first. Ties mean war, and the burn law is exact: the round line ends in `-> war`; then each player lays face-down cards — three if they can, otherwise all but their last card — and flips one more face-up card. Print ` war: A burns , B burns ` then ` A vs B -> ...` resolved like a battle (the whole pile counts toward the take); another tie means another war, same indentation. A player whose deck is EMPTY when a war starts (they tied with their final card) can't fight: print ` war: A has no cards left` (or B), the opponent wins on the spot; if both are empty print ` war: nobody has cards left` and the game is a draw. Game over when someone's deck is empty after a round: `A wins in N rounds` / `B wins in N rounds` / `draw after N rounds` — singular `round` when N is 1. Exit 0 for any completed game. `zsh test_warcard.zsh` is the gate: it diffs complete round-by-round transcripts for hand-built decks (double wars, zero-card burns, last-card ties) plus one full 52-card deal, byte for byte. Don't touch the test file.
